input.prompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a .NET MAUI issue where the keyboard covers the text input at the bottom of the screen. They mention that `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` worked in .NET 9 but doesn't work in .NET 10. This is a known change in .NET MAUI - let me provide guidance based on my knowledge.

In .NET MAUI for .NET 9 and earlier, on Android, you could use `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` to make the layout resize when the keyboard appeared. However, this API was deprecated in Android 30+ (Android 11+), and in .NET 10, MAUI moved away from it.


For .NET 10, the best solution is to use `KeyboardAutoManagerScroll`, which automatically scrolls content into view when the keyboard appears, replacing the deprecated resize approach. This is the recommended way to handle keyboard avoidance going forward. The `KeyboardAutoManagerScroll` feature should handle most keyboard scenarios automatically since it's enabled by default in .NET 8+. For chat-style layouts with a fixed input at the bottom, I'd need to listen to keyboard insets and adjust padding accordingly, or fall back to the deprecated `adjustResize` mode—though that's not reliable on Android 15+ targeting API 35.

The real solution for .NET 10 MAUI is leveraging window insets, specifically the `ime()` insets, to dynamically add bottom padding when the keyboard appears, since Android deprecated the old resize behavior and MAUI's `KeyboardAutoManagerScroll` doesn't fully handle fixed-bottom layouts.
